description: Fix two remaining bugs in the side panel data flow: (1) plugin.uninstall notifications from the server are silently dropped because the handler requires a JSON-RPC id, so adapter cleanup never runs on server-initiated uninstalls; (2) optimistic rollback on tool toggle failure overwrites the entire plugins array with a stale snapshot, clobbering any concurrent server state updates.

userStories.title: Fix plugin.uninstall to work as both notification and request
userStories.description: As a user, I want plugin adapters to be properly cleaned up from matching tabs when I remove a plugin, so that stale adapter code doesn't keep running in the background.
acceptanceCriteria: The MCP server sends plugin.uninstall as a JSON-RPC request (with id) instead of a notification, and waits for the extension's response before sending plugins.changed
handlePluginUninstall in message-router.ts continues to work with wrapAsync (which requires id), and sends its existing JSON-RPC response back to the server

The server's handlePluginRemove in extension-handlers.ts sends plugin.uninstall with an id, awaits the extension's response (with a timeout), then proceeds with plugins.changed and the client response
If the extension doesn't respond within 5 seconds (timeout), the server proceeds anyway (best-effort cleanup)
Existing unit tests in message-router.test.ts for plugin.uninstall are updated — the test that asserts 'does not dispatch without id' is removed or updated since the server now always sends an id
New unit test in extension-handlers.test.ts verifies plugin.uninstall is sent as a request (with id), not a notification
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: The root cause: in `platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts` line 751-755, `sendToExtension` sends `plugin.uninstall` without an `id` field, making it a JSON-RPC notification. But in `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts` line 545, `handlePluginUninstall` is wrapped with `wrapAsync` which silently drops messages without `id` (line 78-84). The server needs to send it as a request. Use the existing `dispatchToExtension` pattern or create a new `sendRequestToExtension` helper in extension-protocol.ts that generates an id and returns a Promise. The server should use `queryExtension` (already exists from US-012 — used for extension.getTabState) to send plugin.uninstall as a request: `queryExtension(state, 'plugin.uninstall', { name: pluginName }, 5000)`. But wait — `queryExtension` expects a response with `result`, and `handlePluginUninstall` sends back a JSON-RPC response via `sendToServer`. Verify that `queryExtension`'s response matching works for this (it checks `data.id` match and `!data.method`). The `handlePluginUninstall` function sends `{ jsonrpc: '2.0', result: { success: true }, id }` — this matches queryExtension's pattern. So the fix on the server side is: `await queryExtension(state, 'plugin.uninstall', { name: pluginName }, 5000).catch(() => {})` (ignore timeout/error — best effort). On the extension side, no change needed — the handler already works with an id.

userStories.title: Fix optimistic rollback to only revert the specific changed field instead of the entire array
userStories.description: As a user, I want tool toggle failures to only revert the toggle I changed, not clobber unrelated server state updates that arrived concurrently.
acceptanceCriteria: handleBgSetToolEnabled rollback re-reads the current cache and only reverts the specific tool's enabled state, not the entire plugins array
handleBgSetAllToolsEnabled rollback re-reads the current cache and only reverts the specific plugin's tools enabled states
handleBgSetBrowserToolEnabled rollback re-reads the current cache and only reverts the specific browser tool's enabled state
handleBgSetAllBrowserToolsEnabled rollback re-reads the current cache and only reverts the specific browser tools enabled states
Concurrent plugins.changed updates that arrive between the optimistic update and the rollback are preserved
Unit tests in background-message-handlers.test.ts verify: (1) rollback only reverts the target tool, not the entire array, (2) a plugins.changed that arrives mid-flight is not clobbered by rollback
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: In `platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts`, four handlers need the same fix pattern. (1) `handleBgSetToolEnabled` (line 320): instead of storing `originalPlugins` and restoring the entire array, the rollback should: `const currentCache = getServerStateCache(); const revertedPlugins = currentCache.plugins.map(p => p.name !== plugin ? p : { ...p, tools: p.tools.map(t => t.name === tool ? { ...t, enabled: !enabled } : t) }); updateServerStateCache({ plugins: revertedPlugins });`. This reads the CURRENT cache (which may have been updated by a concurrent plugins.changed) and only flips the one tool back. Remove the `originalPlugins` variable. (2) `handleBgSetAllToolsEnabled` (line 352): same pattern — re-read current cache, find the plugin, flip all its tools back to `!enabled`. Remove `originalPlugins`. (3) `handleBgSetBrowserToolEnabled` (line 386): re-read current cache, find the browser tool, flip it back. Remove `originalBrowserTools`. (4) `handleBgSetAllBrowserToolsEnabled` (line 411): re-read current cache, flip all browser tools back to `!enabled`. Remove `originalBrowserTools`. Note: this approach uses `!enabled` for the revert which was flagged in a previous review as incorrect for concurrent toggles. But the `pendingOptimisticUpdates` mechanism (added in the previous round) ensures that while the request is pending, any incoming plugins.changed will re-apply the optimistic value. The rollback only runs after `removePendingPluginToolUpdate`, so at that point no pending flag exists and the `!enabled` value is correct (it's the pre-toggle state). The concurrent toggle scenario is handled by the counter-based mechanism — a newer toggle bumps the counter and its own pending flag replaces the older one.
